the_signal.headline: Error handling in tool calling is your new production bottleneck
the_signal.body: n8n's architectural guide shows that LLM tool-calling failures—classification, retries, fallbacks, circuit breakers—are where agents break in production, not in the model itself. If your agent failures aren't traced back to tool-call errors, you're debugging the wrong layer. This shifts reliability ownership from prompt engineering to infrastructure design.

tool_drop.name: Ship OS by Notion
tool_drop.what: Notion's OS-like interface for managing workflows and agent orchestration.
tool_drop.why: GTM teams using Notion for ops could now build lightweight agent workflows without leaving their existing workspace, reducing tool sprawl in revenue stacks.

thread_of_day.title: Building a real-time AI tutor for 5-year-olds
thread_of_day.takeaway: Getting an AI system to reliably teach a child requires solving for intent clarity, context persistence, and error recovery at scales smaller teams ignore—same constraints your revenue agents face.

quote.text: Agent Identity, Reliable Execution, and Intent are only half-way solved

whats_moving.context: As agents delegate to external tools via Model Context Protocol, auth, tool-call scoping, and observability become GTM-critical—one leaked credential breaks your entire agent-driven pipeline.
Token budgeting and context rot management are now production reliability levers, not optimization afterthoughts—your agent's accuracy degrades predictably as conversation history grows.
RAG + threat intelligence + historical incident memory is becoming a solved pattern; GTM teams using this for customer escalation workflows now have architectural blueprints instead of guessing.

act_on_this: Audit your agent's tool-calling error handlers today—classify which failures are retryable, which need fallbacks, and which should trigger circuit breakers; document each in your runbook.

numbers.stat: 75 capabilities expected in an agent development tool (per n8n's analysis)
numbers.context: Most teams are shipping agents with <30% of these capabilities implemented, explaining why production reliability is still a surprise.
